ayahs.textArabic: صٓۚ وَٱلۡقُرۡءَانِ ذِي ٱلذِّكۡرِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Sad waalqurani thee alththikri
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Sad. By the Qur'an containing reminder...
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Ṣãd. By the Quran, full of reminders!

ayahs.textArabic: بَلِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ فِي عِزَّةٖ وَشِقَاقٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Bali allatheena kafaroo fee AAizzatin washiqaqin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But those who disbelieve are in pride and dissension.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹This is the truth,˺ yet the disbelievers are ˹entrenched˺ in arrogance and opposition.

ayahs.textArabic: كَمۡ أَهۡلَكۡنَا مِن قَبۡلِهِم مِّن قَرۡنٖ فَنَادَواْ وَّلَاتَ حِينَ مَنَاصٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kam ahlakna min qablihim min qarnin fanadaw walata heena manasin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: How many a generation have We destroyed before them, and they [then] called out; but it was not a time for escape.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Imagine˺ how many peoples We destroyed before them, and they cried out when it was too late to escape.

ayahs.textArabic: وَعَجِبُوٓاْ أَن جَآءَهُم مُّنذِرٞ مِّنۡهُمۡۖ وَقَالَ ٱلۡكَٰفِرُونَ هَٰذَا سَٰحِرٞ كَذَّابٌ
ayahs.textTransliteration: WaAAajiboo an jaahum munthirun minhum waqala alkafiroona hatha sahirun kaththabun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they wonder that there has come to them a warner from among themselves. And the disbelievers say, "This is a magician and a liar.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Now, the pagans are astonished that a warner has come to them from among themselves. And the disbelievers say, “This is a magician, a total liar!

ayahs.textArabic: أَجَعَلَ ٱلۡأٓلِهَةَ إِلَٰهٗا وَٰحِدًاۖ إِنَّ هَٰذَا لَشَيۡءٌ عُجَابٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: AjaAAala alalihata ilahan wahidan inna hatha lashayon AAujabun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Has he made the gods [only] one God? Indeed, this is a curious thing."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Has he reduced ˹all˺ the gods to One God? Indeed, this is something totally astonishing.”

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱنطَلَقَ ٱلۡمَلَأُ مِنۡهُمۡ أَنِ ٱمۡشُواْ وَٱصۡبِرُواْ عَلَىٰٓ ءَالِهَتِكُمۡۖ إِنَّ هَٰذَا لَشَيۡءٞ يُرَادُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waintalaqa almalao minhum ani imshoo waisbiroo AAala alihatikum inna hatha lashayon yuradu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the eminent among them went forth, [saying], "Continue, and be patient over [the defense of] your gods. Indeed, this is a thing intended.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The chiefs among them went forth saying, “Carry on, and stand firm in devotion to your gods. Certainly this is just a scheme ˹for power˺.

ayahs.textArabic: مَا سَمِعۡنَا بِهَٰذَا فِي ٱلۡمِلَّةِ ٱلۡأٓخِرَةِ إِنۡ هَٰذَآ إِلَّا ٱخۡتِلَٰقٌ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ma samiAAna bihatha fee almillati alakhirati in hatha illa ikhtilaqun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: We have not heard of this in the latest religion. This is not but a fabrication.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We have never heard of this in the previous faith. This is nothing but a fabrication.

ayahs.textArabic: أَءُنزِلَ عَلَيۡهِ ٱلذِّكۡرُ مِنۢ بَيۡنِنَاۚ بَلۡ هُمۡ فِي شَكّٖ مِّن ذِكۡرِيۚ بَل لَّمَّا يَذُوقُواْ عَذَابِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Aonzila AAalayhi alththikru min baynina bal hum fee shakkin min thikree bal lamma yathooqoo AAathabi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Has the message been revealed to him out of [all of] us?" Rather, they are in doubt about My message. Rather, they have not yet tasted My punishment.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Has the Reminder been revealed ˹only˺ to him out of ˹all of˺ us?” In fact, they are ˹only˺ in doubt of My ˹revealed˺ Reminder. In fact, ˹they do so because˺ they have not yet tasted My punishment.

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ عِندَهُمۡ خَزَآئِنُ رَحۡمَةِ رَبِّكَ ٱلۡعَزِيزِ ٱلۡوَهَّابِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am AAindahum khazainu rahmati rabbika alAAazeezi alwahhabi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or do they have the depositories of the mercy of your Lord, the Exalted in Might, the Bestower?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or ˹is it because˺ they possess the treasuries of the mercy of your Lord—the Almighty, the Giver ˹of all bounties˺.

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ لَهُم مُّلۡكُ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَمَا بَيۡنَهُمَاۖ فَلۡيَرۡتَقُواْ فِي ٱلۡأَسۡبَٰبِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am lahum mulku alssamawati waalardi wama baynahuma falyartaqoo fee alasbabi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or is theirs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and what is between them? Then let them ascend through [any] ways of access.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or ˹is it because˺ the kingdom of the heavens and the earth and everything in between belongs to them? Let them then climb their way ˹to heaven, if their claim is true˺.

ayahs.textArabic: جُندٞ مَّا هُنَالِكَ مَهۡزُومٞ مِّنَ ٱلۡأَحۡزَابِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Jundun ma hunalika mahzoomun mina alahzabi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [They are but] soldiers [who will be] defeated there among the companies [of disbelievers].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: This is just another ˹enemy˺ force bound for defeat out there.

ayahs.textArabic: كَذَّبَتۡ قَبۡلَهُمۡ قَوۡمُ نُوحٖ وَعَادٞ وَفِرۡعَوۡنُ ذُو ٱلۡأَوۡتَادِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kaththabat qablahum qawmu noohin waAAadun wafirAAawnu thoo alawtadi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The people of Noah denied before them, and [the tribe of] 'Aad and Pharaoh, the owner of stakes,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Before them, the people of Noah denied ˹the truth˺, as did ’Âd, Pharaoh of the mighty structures,

ayahs.textArabic: وَثَمُودُ وَقَوۡمُ لُوطٖ وَأَصۡحَٰبُ لۡـَٔيۡكَةِۚ أُوْلَٰٓئِكَ ٱلۡأَحۡزَابُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wathamoodu waqawmu lootin waashabu alaykati olaika alahzabu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [the tribe of] Thamud and the people of Lot and the companions of the thicket. Those are the companies.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Thamûd, the people of Lot, and the residents of the Forest. These were ˹all˺ enemy forces.

ayahs.textArabic: إِن كُلٌّ إِلَّا كَذَّبَ ٱلرُّسُلَ فَحَقَّ عِقَابِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: In kullun illa kaththaba alrrusula fahaqqa AAiqabi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Each of them denied the messengers, so My penalty was justified.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Each rejected their messenger, so My punishment was justified.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَا يَنظُرُ هَٰٓؤُلَآءِ إِلَّا صَيۡحَةٗ وَٰحِدَةٗ مَّا لَهَا مِن فَوَاقٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama yanthuru haolai illa sayhatan wahidatan ma laha min fawaqin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And these [disbelievers] await not but one blast [of the Horn]; for it there will be no delay.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: These ˹pagans˺ are awaiting nothing but a single Blast that cannot be stopped.

ayahs.textArabic: وَقَالُواْ رَبَّنَا عَجِّل لَّنَا قِطَّنَا قَبۡلَ يَوۡمِ ٱلۡحِسَابِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqaloo rabbana AAajjil lana qittana qabla yawmi alhisabi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they say, "Our Lord, hasten for us our share [of the punishment] before the Day of Account"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They say ˹mockingly˺, “Our Lord! Hasten for us our share ˹of the punishment˺ before the Day of Reckoning.”

ayahs.textArabic: ٱصۡبِرۡ عَلَىٰ مَا يَقُولُونَ وَٱذۡكُرۡ عَبۡدَنَا دَاوُۥدَ ذَا ٱلۡأَيۡدِۖ إِنَّهُۥٓ أَوَّابٌ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Isbir AAala ma yaqooloona waothkur AAabdana dawooda tha alaydi innahu awwabun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Be patient over what they say and remember Our servant, David, the possessor of strength; indeed, he was one who repeatedly turned back [to Allah].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Be patient ˹O Prophet˺ with what they say. And remember Our servant, David, the man of strength. Indeed, he ˹constantly˺ turned ˹to Allah˺.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّا سَخَّرۡنَا ٱلۡجِبَالَ مَعَهُۥ يُسَبِّحۡنَ بِٱلۡعَشِيِّ وَٱلۡإِشۡرَاقِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna sakhkharna aljibala maAAahu yusabbihna bialAAashiyyi waalishraqi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, We subjected the mountains [to praise] with him, exalting [Allah] in the [late] afternoon and [after] sunrise.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We truly subjected the mountains to hymn ˹Our praises˺ along with him in the evening and after sunrise.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلطَّيۡرَ مَحۡشُورَةٗۖ كُلّٞ لَّهُۥٓ أَوَّابٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waalttayra mahshooratan kullun lahu awwabun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the birds were assembled, all with him repeating [praises].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And ˹We subjected˺ the birds, flocking together. All turned to him ˹echoing his hymns˺.

ayahs.textArabic: وَشَدَدۡنَا مُلۡكَهُۥ وَءَاتَيۡنَٰهُ ٱلۡحِكۡمَةَ وَفَصۡلَ ٱلۡخِطَابِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Washadadna mulkahu waataynahu alhikmata wafasla alkhitabi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We strengthened his kingdom and gave him wisdom and discernment in speech.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We strengthened his kingship, and gave him wisdom and sound judgment.

ayahs.textArabic: ۞ وَهَلۡ أَتَىٰكَ نَبَؤُاْ ٱلۡخَصۡمِ إِذۡ تَسَوَّرُواْ ٱلۡمِحۡرَابَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wahal ataka nabao alkhasmi ith tasawwaroo almihraba
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And has there come to you the news of the adversaries, when they climbed over the wall of [his] prayer chamber -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Has the story of the two plaintiffs, who scaled the ˹wall of David’s˺ sanctuary, reached you ˹O Prophet˺?

ayahs.textArabic: إِذۡ دَخَلُواْ عَلَىٰ دَاوُۥدَ فَفَزِعَ مِنۡهُمۡۖ قَالُواْ لَا تَخَفۡۖ خَصۡمَانِ بَغَىٰ بَعۡضُنَا عَلَىٰ بَعۡضٖ فَٱحۡكُم بَيۡنَنَا بِٱلۡحَقِّ وَلَا تُشۡطِطۡ وَٱهۡدِنَآ إِلَىٰ سَوَآءِ ٱلصِّرَٰطِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ith dakhaloo AAala dawooda fafaziAAa minhum qaloo la takhaf khasmani bagha baAAduna AAala baAAdin faohkum baynana bialhaqqi wala tushtit waihdina ila sawai alssirati
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: When they entered upon David and he was alarmed by them? They said, "Fear not. [We are] two adversaries, one of whom has wronged the other, so judge between us with truth and do not exceed [it] and guide us to the sound path.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: When they came into David’s presence, he was startled by them. They said, “Have no fear. ˹We are merely˺ two in a dispute: one of us has wronged the other. So judge between us with truth—do not go beyond ˹it˺—and guide us to the right way.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ هَٰذَآ أَخِي لَهُۥ تِسۡعٞ وَتِسۡعُونَ نَعۡجَةٗ وَلِيَ نَعۡجَةٞ وَٰحِدَةٞ فَقَالَ أَكۡفِلۡنِيهَا وَعَزَّنِي فِي ٱلۡخِطَابِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna hatha akhee lahu tisAAun watisAAoona naAAjatan waliya naAAjatun wahidatun faqala akfilneeha waAAazzanee fee alkhitabi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed this, my brother, has ninety-nine ewes, and I have one ewe; so he said, 'Entrust her to me,' and he overpowered me in speech."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: This is my brother. He has ninety-nine sheep while I have ˹only˺ one. ˹Still˺ he asked me to give it up to him, overwhelming me with ˹his˺ argument.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ لَقَدۡ ظَلَمَكَ بِسُؤَالِ نَعۡجَتِكَ إِلَىٰ نِعَاجِهِۦۖ وَإِنَّ كَثِيرٗا مِّنَ ٱلۡخُلَطَآءِ لَيَبۡغِي بَعۡضُهُمۡ عَلَىٰ بَعۡضٍ إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَعَمِلُواْ ٱلصَّٰلِحَٰتِ وَقَلِيلٞ مَّا هُمۡۗ وَظَنَّ دَاوُۥدُ أَنَّمَا فَتَنَّٰهُ فَٱسۡتَغۡفَرَ رَبَّهُۥ وَخَرَّۤ رَاكِعٗاۤ وَأَنَابَ۩
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala laqad thalamaka bisuali naAAjatika ila niAAajihi wainna katheeran mina alkhulatai layabghee baAAduhum AAala baAAdin illa allatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati waqaleelun ma hum wathanna dawoodu annama fatannahu faistaghfara rabbahu wakharra rakiAAan waanaba
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [David] said, "He has certainly wronged you in demanding your ewe [in addition] to his ewes. And indeed, many associates oppress one another, except for those who believe and do righteous deeds - and few are they." And David became certain that We had tried him, and he asked forgiveness of his Lord and fell down bowing [in prostration] and turned in repentance [to Allah].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: David ˹eventually˺ ruled, “He has definitely wronged you in demanding ˹to add˺ your sheep to his. And certainly many partners wrong each other, except those who believe and do good—but how few are they!” Then David realized that We had tested him so he asked for his Lord’s forgiveness, fell down in prostration, and turned ˹to Him in repentance˺.

ayahs.textArabic: فَغَفَرۡنَا لَهُۥ ذَٰلِكَۖ وَإِنَّ لَهُۥ عِندَنَا لَزُلۡفَىٰ وَحُسۡنَ مَـَٔابٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faghafarna lahu thalika wainna lahu AAindana lazulfa wahusna maabin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So We forgave him that; and indeed, for him is nearness to Us and a good place of return.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So We forgave that for him. And he will indeed have ˹a status of˺ closeness to Us and an honourable destination!

ayahs.textArabic: يَٰدَاوُۥدُ إِنَّا جَعَلۡنَٰكَ خَلِيفَةٗ فِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِ فَٱحۡكُم بَيۡنَ ٱلنَّاسِ بِٱلۡحَقِّ وَلَا تَتَّبِعِ ٱلۡهَوَىٰ فَيُضِلَّكَ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِۚ إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَضِلُّونَ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ لَهُمۡ عَذَابٞ شَدِيدُۢ بِمَا نَسُواْ يَوۡمَ ٱلۡحِسَابِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ya dawoodu inna jaAAalnaka khaleefatan fee alardi faohkum bayna alnnasi bialhaqqi wala tattabiAAi alhawa fayudillaka AAan sabeeli Allahi inna allatheena yadilloona AAan sabeeli Allahi lahum AAathabun shadeedun bima nasoo yawma alhisabi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [We said], "O David, indeed We have made you a successor upon the earth, so judge between the people in truth and do not follow [your own] desire, as it will lead you astray from the way of Allah." Indeed, those who go astray from the way of Allah will have a severe punishment for having forgotten the Day of Account.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹We instructed him:˺ “O David! We have surely made you an authority in the land, so judge between people with truth. And do not follow ˹your˺ desires or they will lead you astray from Allah’s Way. Surely those who go astray from Allah’s Way will suffer a severe punishment for neglecting the Day of Reckoning.”

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَا خَلَقۡنَا ٱلسَّمَآءَ وَٱلۡأَرۡضَ وَمَا بَيۡنَهُمَا بَٰطِلٗاۚ ذَٰلِكَ ظَنُّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْۚ فَوَيۡلٞ لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ مِنَ ٱلنَّارِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama khalaqna alssamaa waalarda wama baynahuma batilan thalika thannu allatheena kafaroo fawaylun lillatheena kafaroo mina alnnari
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We did not create the heaven and the earth and that between them aimlessly. That is the assumption of those who disbelieve, so woe to those who disbelieve from the Fire.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We have not created the heavens and earth and everything in between without purpose—as the disbelievers think. So woe to the disbelievers because of the Fire!

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ نَجۡعَلُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَعَمِلُواْ ٱلصَّٰلِحَٰتِ كَٱلۡمُفۡسِدِينَ فِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِ أَمۡ نَجۡعَلُ ٱلۡمُتَّقِينَ كَٱلۡفُجَّارِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am najAAalu allatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati kaalmufsideena fee alardi am najAAalu almuttaqeena kaalfujjari
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or should we treat those who believe and do righteous deeds like corrupters in the land? Or should We treat those who fear Allah like the wicked?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or should We treat those who believe and do good like those who make mischief throughout the land? Or should We treat the righteous like the wicked?

ayahs.textArabic: كِتَٰبٌ أَنزَلۡنَٰهُ إِلَيۡكَ مُبَٰرَكٞ لِّيَدَّبَّرُوٓاْ ءَايَٰتِهِۦ وَلِيَتَذَكَّرَ أُوْلُواْ ٱلۡأَلۡبَٰبِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kitabun anzalnahu ilayka mubarakun liyaddabbaroo ayatihi waliyatathakkara oloo alalbabi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [This is] a blessed Book which We have revealed to you, [O Muhammad], that they might reflect upon its verses and that those of understanding would be reminded.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹This is˺ a blessed Book which We have revealed to you ˹O Prophet˺ so that they may contemplate its verses, and people of reason may be mindful.

ayahs.textArabic: وَوَهَبۡنَا لِدَاوُۥدَ سُلَيۡمَٰنَۚ نِعۡمَ ٱلۡعَبۡدُ إِنَّهُۥٓ أَوَّابٌ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wawahabna lidawooda sulaymana niAAma alAAabdu innahu awwabun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And to David We gave Solomon. An excellent servant, indeed he was one repeatedly turning back [to Allah].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We blessed David with Solomon—what an excellent servant ˹he was˺! Indeed, he ˹constantly˺ turned ˹to Allah˺.

ayahs.textArabic: إِذۡ عُرِضَ عَلَيۡهِ بِٱلۡعَشِيِّ ٱلصَّٰفِنَٰتُ ٱلۡجِيَادُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ith AAurida AAalayhi bialAAashiyyi alssafinatu aljiyadu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Mention] when there were exhibited before him in the afternoon the poised [standing] racehorses.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Remember˺ when the well-trained, swift horses were paraded before him in the evening.

ayahs.textArabic: فَقَالَ إِنِّيٓ أَحۡبَبۡتُ حُبَّ ٱلۡخَيۡرِ عَن ذِكۡرِ رَبِّي حَتَّىٰ تَوَارَتۡ بِٱلۡحِجَابِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faqala innee ahbabtu hubba alkhayri AAan thikri rabbee hatta tawarat bialhijabi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And he said, "Indeed, I gave preference to the love of good [things] over the remembrance of my Lord until the sun disappeared into the curtain [of darkness]."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He then proclaimed, “I am truly in love with ˹these˺ fine things out of remembrance for Allah,” until they went out of sight.

ayahs.textArabic: رُدُّوهَا عَلَيَّۖ فَطَفِقَ مَسۡحَۢا بِٱلسُّوقِ وَٱلۡأَعۡنَاقِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ruddooha AAalayya fatafiqa mashan bialssooqi waalaAAnaqi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [He said], "Return them to me," and set about striking [their] legs and necks.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹He ordered,˺ “Bring them back to me!” Then he began to rub down their legs and necks.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ فَتَنَّا سُلَيۡمَٰنَ وَأَلۡقَيۡنَا عَلَىٰ كُرۡسِيِّهِۦ جَسَدٗا ثُمَّ أَنَابَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad fatanna sulaymana waalqayna AAala kursiyyihi jasadan thumma anaba
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We certainly tried Solomon and placed on his throne a body; then he returned.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And indeed, We tested Solomon, placing a ˹deformed˺ body on his throne, then he turned ˹to Allah in repentance˺.

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ رَبِّ ٱغۡفِرۡ لِي وَهَبۡ لِي مُلۡكٗا لَّا يَنۢبَغِي لِأَحَدٖ مِّنۢ بَعۡدِيٓۖ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ ٱلۡوَهَّابُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala rabbi ighfir lee wahab lee mulkan la yanbaghee liahadin min baAAdee innaka anta alwahhabu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "My Lord, forgive me and grant me a kingdom such as will not belong to anyone after me. Indeed, You are the Bestower."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He prayed, “My Lord! Forgive me, and grant me an authority that will never be matched by anyone after me. You are indeed the Giver ˹of all bounties˺.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَسَخَّرۡنَا لَهُ ٱلرِّيحَ تَجۡرِي بِأَمۡرِهِۦ رُخَآءً حَيۡثُ أَصَابَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fasakhkharna lahu alrreeha tajree biamrihi rukhaan haythu asaba
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So We subjected to him the wind blowing by his command, gently, wherever he directed,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So We subjected to him the wind, blowing gently at his command to wherever he pleased.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلشَّيَٰطِينَ كُلَّ بَنَّآءٖ وَغَوَّاصٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waalshshayateena kulla bannain waghawwasin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [also] the devils [of jinn] - every builder and diver
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And ˹We subjected to him˺ every builder and diver of the jinn,

ayahs.textArabic: وَءَاخَرِينَ مُقَرَّنِينَ فِي ٱلۡأَصۡفَادِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waakhareena muqarraneena fee alasfadi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And others bound together in shackles.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and others bound together in chains.

ayahs.textArabic: هَٰذَا عَطَآؤُنَا فَٱمۡنُنۡ أَوۡ أَمۡسِكۡ بِغَيۡرِ حِسَابٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Hatha AAataona faomnun aw amsik bighayri hisabin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [We said], "This is Our gift, so grant or withhold without account."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Allah said,˺ “This is Our gift, so give or withhold ˹as you wish˺, never to be called to account.”

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِنَّ لَهُۥ عِندَنَا لَزُلۡفَىٰ وَحُسۡنَ مَـَٔابٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wainna lahu AAindana lazulfa wahusna maabin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, for him is nearness to Us and a good place of return.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And he will indeed have ˹a status of˺ closeness to Us and an honourable destination!

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱذۡكُرۡ عَبۡدَنَآ أَيُّوبَ إِذۡ نَادَىٰ رَبَّهُۥٓ أَنِّي مَسَّنِيَ ٱلشَّيۡطَٰنُ بِنُصۡبٖ وَعَذَابٍ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waothkur AAabdana ayyooba ith nada rabbahu annee massaniya alshshaytanu binusbin waAAathabin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And remember Our servant Job, when he called to his Lord, "Indeed, Satan has touched me with hardship and torment."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And remember Our servant Job, when he cried out to his Lord, “Satan has afflicted me with distress and suffering.”

ayahs.textArabic: ٱرۡكُضۡ بِرِجۡلِكَۖ هَٰذَا مُغۡتَسَلُۢ بَارِدٞ وَشَرَابٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Orkud birijlika hatha mughtasalun baridun washarabun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [So he was told], "Strike [the ground] with your foot; this is a [spring for] a cool bath and drink."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹We responded,˺ “Stomp your foot: ˹now˺ here is a cool ˹and refreshing˺ spring for washing and drinking.”

ayahs.textArabic: وَوَهَبۡنَا لَهُۥٓ أَهۡلَهُۥ وَمِثۡلَهُم مَّعَهُمۡ رَحۡمَةٗ مِّنَّا وَذِكۡرَىٰ لِأُوْلِي ٱلۡأَلۡبَٰبِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wawahabna lahu ahlahu wamithlahum maAAahum rahmatan minna wathikra liolee alalbabi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We granted him his family and a like [number] with them as mercy from Us and a reminder for those of understanding.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We gave him back his family, twice as many, as a mercy from Us and a lesson for people of reason.

ayahs.textArabic: وَخُذۡ بِيَدِكَ ضِغۡثٗا فَٱضۡرِب بِّهِۦ وَلَا تَحۡنَثۡۗ إِنَّا وَجَدۡنَٰهُ صَابِرٗاۚ نِّعۡمَ ٱلۡعَبۡدُ إِنَّهُۥٓ أَوَّابٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wakhuth biyadika dighthan faidrib bihi wala tahnath inna wajadnahu sabiran niAAma alAAabdu innahu awwabun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [We said], "And take in your hand a bunch [of grass] and strike with it and do not break your oath." Indeed, We found him patient, an excellent servant. Indeed, he was one repeatedly turning back [to Allah].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹And We said to him,˺ “Take in your hand a bundle of grass, and strike ˹your wife˺ with it, and do not break your oath.” We truly found him patient. What an excellent servant ˹he was˺! Indeed, he ˹constantly˺ turned ˹to Allah˺.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱذۡكُرۡ عِبَٰدَنَآ إِبۡرَٰهِيمَ وَإِسۡحَٰقَ وَيَعۡقُوبَ أُوْلِي ٱلۡأَيۡدِي وَٱلۡأَبۡصَٰرِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waothkur AAibadana ibraheema waishaqa wayaAAqooba olee alaydee waalabsari
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And remember Our servants, Abraham, Isaac and Jacob - those of strength and [religious] vision.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And remember Our servants: Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob—the men of strength and insight.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّآ أَخۡلَصۡنَٰهُم بِخَالِصَةٖ ذِكۡرَى ٱلدَّارِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna akhlasnahum bikhalisatin thikra alddari
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, We chose them for an exclusive quality: remembrance of the home [of the Hereafter].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We truly chose them for the honour of proclaiming the Hereafter.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِنَّهُمۡ عِندَنَا لَمِنَ ٱلۡمُصۡطَفَيۡنَ ٱلۡأَخۡيَارِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wainnahum AAindana lamina almustafayna alakhyari
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed they are, to Us, among the chosen and outstanding.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And in Our sight they are truly among the chosen and the finest.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱذۡكُرۡ إِسۡمَٰعِيلَ وَٱلۡيَسَعَ وَذَا ٱلۡكِفۡلِۖ وَكُلّٞ مِّنَ ٱلۡأَخۡيَارِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waothkur ismaAAeela wailyasaAAa watha alkifli wakullun mina alakhyari
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And remember Ishmael, Elisha and Dhul-Kifl, and all are among the outstanding.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Also remember Ishmael, Elisha, and Ⱬul-Kifl. All are among the best.

ayahs.textArabic: هَٰذَا ذِكۡرٞۚ وَإِنَّ لِلۡمُتَّقِينَ لَحُسۡنَ مَـَٔابٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Hatha thikrun wainna lilmuttaqeena lahusna maabin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: This is a reminder. And indeed, for the righteous is a good place of return
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: This is ˹all˺ a reminder. And the righteous will certainly have an honourable destination:

ayahs.textArabic: جَنَّٰتِ عَدۡنٖ مُّفَتَّحَةٗ لَّهُمُ ٱلۡأَبۡوَٰبُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Jannati AAadnin mufattahatan lahumu alabwabu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Gardens of perpetual residence, whose doors will be opened to them.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: the Gardens of Eternity, whose gates will be open for them.

ayahs.textArabic: مُتَّكِـِٔينَ فِيهَا يَدۡعُونَ فِيهَا بِفَٰكِهَةٖ كَثِيرَةٖ وَشَرَابٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Muttakieena feeha yadAAoona feeha bifakihatin katheeratin washarabin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Reclining within them, they will call therein for abundant fruit and drink.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: There they will recline, calling for abundant fruit and drink.

ayahs.textArabic: ۞ وَعِندَهُمۡ قَٰصِرَٰتُ ٱلطَّرۡفِ أَتۡرَابٌ
ayahs.textTransliteration: WaAAindahum qasiratu alttarfi atrabun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And with them will be women limiting [their] glances and of equal age.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And with them will be maidens of modest gaze and equal age.

ayahs.textArabic: هَٰذَا مَا تُوعَدُونَ لِيَوۡمِ ٱلۡحِسَابِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Hatha ma tooAAadoona liyawmi alhisabi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: This is what you, [the righteous], are promised for the Day of Account.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: This is what you are promised for the Day of Reckoning.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ هَٰذَا لَرِزۡقُنَا مَا لَهُۥ مِن نَّفَادٍ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna hatha larizquna ma lahu min nafadin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, this is Our provision; for it there is no depletion.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: This is indeed Our provision that will never end.

ayahs.textArabic: هَٰذَاۚ وَإِنَّ لِلطَّٰغِينَ لَشَرَّ مَـَٔابٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Hatha wainna lilttagheena lasharra maabin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: This [is so]. But indeed, for the transgressors is an evil place of return -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: That is that. And the transgressors will certainly have the worst destination:

ayahs.textArabic: جَهَنَّمَ يَصۡلَوۡنَهَا فَبِئۡسَ ٱلۡمِهَادُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Jahannama yaslawnaha fabisa almihadu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Hell, which they will [enter to] burn, and wretched is the resting place.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Hell, where they will burn. What an evil place to rest!

ayahs.textArabic: هَٰذَا فَلۡيَذُوقُوهُ حَمِيمٞ وَغَسَّاقٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Hatha falyathooqoohu hameemun waghassaqun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: This - so let them taste it - is scalding water and [foul] purulence.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Let them then taste this: boiling water and ˹oozing˺ pus,

ayahs.textArabic: وَءَاخَرُ مِن شَكۡلِهِۦٓ أَزۡوَٰجٌ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waakharu min shaklihi azwajun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And other [punishments] of its type [in various] kinds.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and other torments of the same sort!

ayahs.textArabic: هَٰذَا فَوۡجٞ مُّقۡتَحِمٞ مَّعَكُمۡ لَا مَرۡحَبَۢا بِهِمۡۚ إِنَّهُمۡ صَالُواْ ٱلنَّارِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Hatha fawjun muqtahimun maAAakum la marhaban bihim innahum saloo alnnari
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Its inhabitants will say], "This is a company bursting in with you. No welcome for them. Indeed, they will burn in the Fire."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹The misleaders will say to one another,˺ “Here is a crowd ˹of followers˺ being thrown in with us. They are not welcome, ˹for˺ they ˹too˺ will burn in the Fire.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُواْ بَلۡ أَنتُمۡ لَا مَرۡحَبَۢا بِكُمۡۖ أَنتُمۡ قَدَّمۡتُمُوهُ لَنَاۖ فَبِئۡسَ ٱلۡقَرَارُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo bal antum la marhaban bikum antum qaddamtumoohu lana fabisa alqararu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will say, "Nor you! No welcome for you. You, [our leaders], brought this upon us, and wretched is the settlement."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The followers will respond, “No! You are not welcome! You brought this upon us. What an evil place for settlement!”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُواْ رَبَّنَا مَن قَدَّمَ لَنَا هَٰذَا فَزِدۡهُ عَذَابٗا ضِعۡفٗا فِي ٱلنَّارِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo rabbana man qaddama lana hatha fazidhu AAathaban diAAfan fee alnnari
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will say, "Our Lord, whoever brought this upon us - increase for him double punishment in the Fire."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Adding, “Our Lord! Whoever brought this upon us, double their punishment in the Fire.”

ayahs.textArabic: وَقَالُواْ مَا لَنَا لَا نَرَىٰ رِجَالٗا كُنَّا نَعُدُّهُم مِّنَ ٱلۡأَشۡرَارِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqaloo ma lana la nara rijalan kunna naAAudduhum mina alashrari
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they will say, "Why do we not see men whom we used to count among the worst?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The tyrants will ask ˹one another˺, “But why do we not see those we considered to be lowly?

ayahs.textArabic: أَتَّخَذۡنَٰهُمۡ سِخۡرِيًّا أَمۡ زَاغَتۡ عَنۡهُمُ ٱلۡأَبۡصَٰرُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Attakhathnahum sikhriyyan am zaghat AAanhumu alabsaru
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Is it [because] we took them in ridicule, or has [our] vision turned away from them?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Were we wrong in mocking them ˹in the world˺? Or do our eyes ˹just˺ fail to see them ˹in the Fire˺?”

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ ذَٰلِكَ لَحَقّٞ تَخَاصُمُ أَهۡلِ ٱلنَّارِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna thalika lahaqqun takhasumu ahli alnnari
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, that is truth - the quarreling of the people of the Fire.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: This dispute between the residents of the Fire will certainly come to pass.

ayahs.textArabic: قُلۡ إِنَّمَآ أَنَا۠ مُنذِرٞۖ وَمَا مِنۡ إِلَٰهٍ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ ٱلۡوَٰحِدُ ٱلۡقَهَّارُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qul innama ana munthirun wama min ilahin illa Allahu alwahidu alqahharu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Say, [O Muhammad], "I am only a warner, and there is not any deity except Allah, the One, the Prevailing.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “I am only a warner. And there is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Allah—the One, the Supreme.

ayahs.textArabic: رَبُّ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَمَا بَيۡنَهُمَا ٱلۡعَزِيزُ ٱلۡغَفَّٰرُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Rabbu alssamawati waalardi wama baynahuma alAAazeezu alghaffaru
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, the Exalted in Might, the Perpetual Forgiver."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹He is the˺ Lord of the heavens and the earth and everything in between—the Almighty, Most Forgiving.”

ayahs.textArabic: قُلۡ هُوَ نَبَؤٌاْ عَظِيمٌ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qul huwa nabaon AAatheemun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Say, "It is great news
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Say, “This ˹Quran˺ is momentous news,

ayahs.textArabic: أَنتُمۡ عَنۡهُ مُعۡرِضُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Antum AAanhu muAAridoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: From which you turn away.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: from which you ˹pagans˺ are turning away.”

ayahs.textArabic: مَا كَانَ لِيَ مِنۡ عِلۡمِۭ بِٱلۡمَلَإِ ٱلۡأَعۡلَىٰٓ إِذۡ يَخۡتَصِمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ma kana liya min AAilmin bialmalai alaAAla ith yakhtasimoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: I had no knowledge of the exalted assembly [of angels] when they were disputing [the creation of Adam].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹And say,˺ “I had no knowledge of the highest assembly ˹in heaven˺ when they differed ˹concerning Adam˺.

ayahs.textArabic: إِن يُوحَىٰٓ إِلَيَّ إِلَّآ أَنَّمَآ أَنَا۠ نَذِيرٞ مُّبِينٌ
ayahs.textTransliteration: In yooha ilayya illa annama ana natheerun mubeenun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: It has not been revealed to me except that I am a clear warner."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: What is revealed to me is that I am only sent with a clear warning.”

ayahs.textArabic: إِذۡ قَالَ رَبُّكَ لِلۡمَلَٰٓئِكَةِ إِنِّي خَٰلِقُۢ بَشَرٗا مِّن طِينٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ith qala rabbuka lilmalaikati innee khaliqun basharan min teenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [So mention] when your Lord said to the angels, "Indeed, I am going to create a human being from clay.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Remember, O Prophet˺ when your Lord said to the angels, “I am going to create a human being from clay.

ayahs.textArabic: فَإِذَا سَوَّيۡتُهُۥ وَنَفَخۡتُ فِيهِ مِن رُّوحِي فَقَعُواْ لَهُۥ سَٰجِدِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faitha sawwaytuhu wanafakhtu feehi min roohee faqaAAoo lahu sajideena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So when I have proportioned him and breathed into him of My [created] soul, then fall down to him in prostration."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So when I have fashioned him and had a spirit of My Own ˹creation˺ breathed into him, fall down in prostration to him.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَسَجَدَ ٱلۡمَلَٰٓئِكَةُ كُلُّهُمۡ أَجۡمَعُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fasajada almalaikatu kulluhum ajmaAAoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So the angels prostrated - all of them entirely.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So the angels prostrated all together—

ayahs.textArabic: إِلَّآ إِبۡلِيسَ ٱسۡتَكۡبَرَ وَكَانَ مِنَ ٱلۡكَٰفِرِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Illa ibleesa istakbara wakana mina alkafireena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Except Iblees; he was arrogant and became among the disbelievers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: but not Iblîs, who acted arrogantly, becoming unfaithful.

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ يَٰٓإِبۡلِيسُ مَا مَنَعَكَ أَن تَسۡجُدَ لِمَا خَلَقۡتُ بِيَدَيَّۖ أَسۡتَكۡبَرۡتَ أَمۡ كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلۡعَالِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala ya ibleesu ma manaAAaka an tasjuda lima khalaqtu biyadayya astakbarta am kunta mina alAAaleena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Allah] said, "O Iblees, what prevented you from prostrating to that which I created with My hands? Were you arrogant [then], or were you [already] among the haughty?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Allah asked, “O Iblîs! What prevented you from prostrating to what I created with My Own Hands? Did you ˹just˺ become proud? Or have you always been arrogant?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ أَنَا۠ خَيۡرٞ مِّنۡهُ خَلَقۡتَنِي مِن نَّارٖ وَخَلَقۡتَهُۥ مِن طِينٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala ana khayrun minhu khalaqtanee min narin wakhalaqtahu min teenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "I am better than him. You created me from fire and created him from clay."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He replied, “I am better than he is: You created me from fire and him from clay.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ فَٱخۡرُجۡ مِنۡهَا فَإِنَّكَ رَجِيمٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala faokhruj minha fainnaka rajeemun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Allah] said, "Then get out of Paradise, for indeed, you are expelled.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Allah commanded, “Then get out of Paradise, for you are truly cursed.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِنَّ عَلَيۡكَ لَعۡنَتِيٓ إِلَىٰ يَوۡمِ ٱلدِّينِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wainna AAalayka laAAnatee ila yawmi alddeeni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, upon you is My curse until the Day of Recompense."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And surely upon you is My condemnation until the Day of Judgment.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ رَبِّ فَأَنظِرۡنِيٓ إِلَىٰ يَوۡمِ يُبۡعَثُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala rabbi faanthirnee ila yawmi yubAAathoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "My Lord, then reprieve me until the Day they are resurrected."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Satan appealed, “My Lord! Then delay my end until the Day of their resurrection.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ فَإِنَّكَ مِنَ ٱلۡمُنظَرِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala fainnaka mina almunthareena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Allah] said, "So indeed, you are of those reprieved
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Allah said, “You will be delayed

ayahs.textArabic: إِلَىٰ يَوۡمِ ٱلۡوَقۡتِ ٱلۡمَعۡلُومِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ila yawmi alwaqti almaAAloomi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Until the Day of the time well-known."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: until the appointed Day.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ فَبِعِزَّتِكَ لَأُغۡوِيَنَّهُمۡ أَجۡمَعِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala fabiAAizzatika laoghwiyannahum ajmaAAeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Iblees] said, "By your might, I will surely mislead them all
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Satan said, “By Your Glory! I will certainly mislead them all,

ayahs.textArabic: إِلَّا عِبَادَكَ مِنۡهُمُ ٱلۡمُخۡلَصِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Illa AAibadaka minhumu almukhlaseena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Except, among them, Your chosen servants."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: except Your chosen servants among them.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ فَٱلۡحَقُّ وَٱلۡحَقَّ أَقُولُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala faalhaqqu waalhaqqa aqoolu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Allah] said, "The truth [is My oath], and the truth I say -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Allah concluded, “The truth is—and I ˹only˺ say the truth—:

ayahs.textArabic: لَأَمۡلَأَنَّ جَهَنَّمَ مِنكَ وَمِمَّن تَبِعَكَ مِنۡهُمۡ أَجۡمَعِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Laamlaanna jahannama minka wamimman tabiAAaka minhum ajmaAAeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [That] I will surely fill Hell with you and those of them that follow you all together."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: I will surely fill up Hell with you and whoever follows you from among them, all together.”

ayahs.textArabic: قُلۡ مَآ أَسۡـَٔلُكُمۡ عَلَيۡهِ مِنۡ أَجۡرٖ وَمَآ أَنَا۠ مِنَ ٱلۡمُتَكَلِّفِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qul ma asalukum AAalayhi min ajrin wama ana mina almutakallifeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Say, [O Muhammad], "I do not ask you for the Qur'an any payment, and I am not of the pretentious
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “I do not ask you for any reward for this ˹Quran˺, nor do I pretend to be someone I am not.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنۡ هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكۡرٞ لِّلۡعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: In huwa illa thikrun lilAAalameena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: It is but a reminder to the worlds.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: It is only a reminder to the whole world.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَتَعۡلَمُنَّ نَبَأَهُۥ بَعۡدَ حِينِۭ
ayahs.textTransliteration: WalataAAlamunna nabaahu baAAda heenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And you will surely know [the truth of] its information after a time."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And you will certainly know its truth before long.”
